ddls:
CREATE TABLE "games" (
  "game_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each game */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title of the game */,
  "console" INTEGER /* Reference to the console the game is available on */,
  "esrb_rating" INTEGER /* ESRB rating of the game */,
  "genre" TEXT /* Genre of the game (e.g., action, adventure, role-playing) */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Release date of the game */,
  "publisher" TEXT /* Publisher of the game */,
  "developer" TEXT /* Developer of the game */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("game_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_games_developer FOREIGN KEY ("developer") REFERENCES developers ("developer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_games_publisher FOREIGN KEY ("publisher") REFERENCES publishers ("publisher_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_games_genre FOREIGN KEY ("genre") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_games_console FOREIGN KEY ("console") REFERENCES consoles ("console_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "content_descriptors" (
  "descriptor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each content descriptor */,
  "descriptor_name" TEXT /* Name of the descriptor (e.g., animated blood, nudity) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the descriptor */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the descriptor (e.g., violence, language, substance use) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("descriptor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "game_content" (
  "game_content_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each mapping entry */,
  "game_id" INTEGER /* ID of the game */,
  "descriptor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the content descriptor associated with the game */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the descriptor in the game (e.g., mild, moderate, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("game_content_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_game_content_descriptor_id FOREIGN KEY ("descriptor_id") REFERENCES content_descriptors ("descriptor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_game_content_game_id FOREIGN KEY ("game_id") REFERENCES games ("game_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "consoles" (
  "console_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each console */,
  "console_name" TEXT /* Name of the console */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the console */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("console_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "publishers" (
  "publisher_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each publisher */,
  "publisher_name" TEXT /* Name of the publisher */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the publisher */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("publisher_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "developers" (
  "developer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each developer */,
  "developer_name" TEXT /* Name of the developer */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the developer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("developer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., content manager, marketing team, compliance officer) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
column_names: Reference to the console the game is available on
Genre of the game (e.g., action, adventure, role-playing)
Unique identifier for each content descriptor
Name of the descriptor (e.g., animated blood, nudity)
Category of the descriptor (e.g., violence, language, substance use)
Unique identifier for each mapping entry
ID of the content descriptor associated with the game
Severity level of the descriptor in the game (e.g., mild, moderate, severe)
Role of the user (e.g., content manager, marketing team, compliance officer)
